entries.title: Tantive IV Capture Opens the Visible Crisis

content.fact: The capture of the Tantive IV exposes Leia to Imperial custody and turns the Death Star plans into an active chase objective.
content.injection: At the ANH opening, Leia is captured, the plans are hidden with the droids, and the Empire is trying to recover them before the Rebels can analyze the station.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Tantive IV Captured.
Respect story-position bounds from Tantive IV Captured to Tantive IV Captured.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Leia Is Both Senatorial Royalty and Rebel Agent

content.fact: Leia’s public identity as Alderaanian princess and senator masks her active role in the Rebel Alliance.
content.injection: Before the Empire exposes her Rebellion links, treat Leia as moving between diplomatic cover and covert Rebel work; ordinary bystanders need not know her operational role.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: A New Hope / Yavin Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Tantive IV Captured to Battle of Yavin.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.fact: R2-D2 carries the Death Star plans, but most characters who encounter him on Tatooine do not know that.
content.injection: Do not have casual Tatooine characters know the droids contain the Death Star plans unless the scene establishes discovery or Rebel context.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Death Star Plans Reach Tatooine.

content.fact: Luke begins the crisis as a frustrated Tatooine farmboy, not as a trained Rebel officer or Jedi.
content.injection: Before Obi-Wan draws him in, write Luke as Force-sensitive but untrained, local, and unaware of the full Rebel/Imperial stakes.

entries.title: Obi-Wan Lives as Ben in Exile

content.fact: Obi-Wan Kenobi lives in hiding as Ben Kenobi and does not openly operate as a Jedi until Luke enters the crisis.
content.injection: In Death Star Plans Reach Tatooine, on Tatooine before the ANH call to action, keep Obi-Wan as a hidden exile; local knowledge may frame him as old Ben rather than a public Jedi master.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Death Star Plans Reach Tatooine.
Respect story-position bounds from Death Star Plans Reach Tatooine to Death Star Plans Reach Tatooine.

entries.title: Lars Deaths Cut Luke’s Tatooine Anchor

context.label: Obi-Wan Draws Luke Into the Rebellion

content.fact: The deaths of Owen and Beru remove Luke’s ordinary home obligation and allow him to leave Tatooine with Obi-Wan.
content.injection: After the Lars homestead is destroyed, Luke’s motivation can turn from escape fantasy to grief, anger, and commitment to stopping the Empire.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Obi-Wan Draws Luke Into the Rebellion.
Respect story-position bounds from Obi-Wan Draws Luke Into the Rebellion to Obi-Wan Draws Luke Into the Rebellion.

entries.title: Mos Eisley Is a Criminal Transit Point

context.label: Obi-Wan Draws Luke Into the Rebellion

content.fact: Mos Eisley is a dangerous Outer Rim spaceport where smugglers, informants, and Imperial attention overlap.
content.injection: In Mos Eisley scenes, use underworld risk, armed strangers, Imperial patrols, and transport bargaining rather than clean civilian travel.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Obi-Wan Draws Luke Into the Rebellion.
Respect story-position bounds from Obi-Wan Draws Luke Into the Rebellion to Obi-Wan Draws Luke Into the Rebellion.

entries.title: Han’s Debt to Jabba Is Active

content.fact: Han owes Jabba and is under pressure from bounty hunters before he commits fully to the Rebel cause.
content.injection: Until Han’s later growth changes his priorities, keep Jabba’s debt and bounty pressure active when underworld or Tatooine context appears.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: A New Hope / Yavin Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Tantive IV Captured to Battle of Yavin.
antiLore: Do not let general model memory override the selected Legends story position.

content.fact: The Millennium Falcon appears battered and suspect but is extremely fast, modified, and capable in dangerous escapes.
content.injection: Use the Falcon as an unreliable-looking but high-performance smuggler ship; mechanical trouble and brilliance can coexist.

content.fact: Alderaan’s destruction makes Leia a survivor of planetary genocide while she remains a Rebel leader under captivity.
content.injection: After Alderaan is destroyed, Leia can still be defiant, but scenes should remember that her homeworld and family have been annihilated.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Alderaan Destroyed.

entries.title: Death Star Is Imperial Terror Doctrine

content.fact: The first Death Star is not merely a large station; it is a doctrine of rule through planetary terror.
content.injection: When the Death Star is active, Imperial command can rely on fear, hostage logic, and demonstration of overwhelming force.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: A New Hope / Yavin Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Tantive IV Captured to Battle of Yavin.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

entries.title: Yavin Attack Depends on Stolen Plans

content.fact: The Battle of Yavin depends on analyzing the stolen plans and exploiting the thermal exhaust port weakness.
content.injection: At Yavin, the Rebels should act from desperate technical intelligence rather than simple fleet strength.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Battle of Yavin.
Respect story-position bounds from Battle of Yavin to Battle of Yavin.
antiLore: Do not let general model memory override the selected Legends story position.

entries.title: Luke Yavin Hero, Not Jedi Knight

content.fact: After Yavin, Luke is a Rebel hero and Force-sensitive pilot, but not a trained Jedi Knight.
content.injection: In post-Yavin/pre-Dagobah context, do not portray Luke as a complete Jedi; his hero status comes from piloting and Force instinct.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Yavin Aftermath.
Respect story-position bounds from Yavin Aftermath to Yavin Aftermath.

entries.title: Han Rewarded but Not Fully Committed Yet

content.fact: After Yavin, Han has helped save the Rebellion but still carries smuggler instincts, debt pressure, and ambivalence about staying.
content.injection: Post-Yavin Han can be loyal in action while still talking like someone who might leave if debt, money, or danger pushes him.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Yavin Aftermath.
Respect story-position bounds from Yavin Aftermath to Yavin Aftermath.
